identity.name: state_read_plane_v1
identity.title: Adopt state-first read-plane architecture slice under control/modules

objective: Adopt a state-first architecture slice rooted in get_state and hydrate_state, with ACP as transport and Marimo as registry/view consumer, and carry it through a bounded kernel review packet.

in_scope: interop matrix for entities, adapters, interfaces, wrappers, transport, and registry boundaries

constraints: The read path is the product and must remain primary.
get_state must remain side-effect free and idempotent.
hydrate_state may refresh and enrich snapshots but may not mutate repositories.
ACP is transport only and may not become the model layer.
Marimo consumes structured state snapshots and hydration metadata only.
gix remains the deterministic Git fact source.
sem remains downstream semantic enrichment over deterministic diff input.
The operator entrypoint is a just module that wraps Python.
assumptions: Lifecycle approvals for this packet tranche are pre-granted through tracking issue #15 unless a new authority boundary is introduced.
Existing git substrate adapters remain available as substrate inputs while the state-first architecture is defined.
control/modules schema family remains the enforcement basis for module artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: control/modules/state exists with module root, inventory, and namespace contract artifacts
workflow DAG is authored before implementation expansion
interop matrix distinguishes entities, adapters, interfaces, wrappers, transport, and registry
packet artifacts are emitted under generated/problem_sets and generated/packets
tracking issue #15 is referenced from the packet basis
review_criteria: The state-first read plane is simpler than a general Git capability surface.
gix and sem remain substrate interfaces rather than authority surfaces.
The packet stays architecture-only and review-only.
